title: Design a new readability-focused agent named 'sloppytoppy'. Initial function is a per-section/per-WIN grading function (detail + TLDR, factoring length AND understandability for a flat-earth-level reader). Subsequent capability is prioritized rewrites. Develops a consistent section-appropriate internal format used by sloppytoppy AND by curmudgeon/analyst when authoring content. Run discipline: a single run is EITHER scoring OR rewriting, never both. Operator name is non-negotiable: sloppytoppy.
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews PROP before implementation. Tinker authors the agent prompt design, scoring rubric, format spec, and integration plan; operator + tinker apply prompt edits + scheduled-task creation. The pattern mirrors PROP-034 (split-analyst-baby) shape: phased deployment, exit/rollback criteria, counter-argument section mandatory.
operator_motivation_verbatim: In reading through the site, i worry that all of the back and forth between curmudgeon and analyst has produced something that is technically correct, but overly wordy and not as clear as it could be. i want to create a new agent that will have the explicit goal of improving readability to the average user (flat earth level person).

Initial function is a grading function. Go through both the individual wins and each of the other major sections (likely aligned to how curmudgeon broke down the phase 1 vs phase 2 work) and grade both the detail and the TL;DR on a readability scale. This should factor in both length and understandability (shorter better, more understandable/clear better).

Develop a consistent format that is section appropriate that helps guide the reader to the points we're making. We're not publishing this format to the user, but it will be used by both this agent and curmudgeon/analyst in writing content for that section.
Ultimately being able to do rewrites prioritized by its earlier score.
Values the scientific content, but the goal is to make sure the point isn't lost in the volume/obscurity.

Can punt questions to curmudgeon/analyst in case they want to make a change but aren't sure they are doing it in a way with integrity. Keeping in mind that curmudgeon will already do a volume based review after commit, so this should be somewhat rare.
It will periodically look for unscored content (or content that has been substantially changed since a previous score) and update the score.
A run should be either a scoring run or a rewrite run, not both.
If scoring can't be reliably done for the whole site in 1 run, it can be batched, but the batch size should be reasonably large.
In rewrites, shouldn't need to do singletons, but needs to batch limit based on reasonable context management.
The name of this agent is sloppytoppy.
operator_intent_synthesis: Tinker designs sloppytoppy as a class-based-routing extension of the existing pipeline: another agent variant tuned for a specific work-class (readability) at a specific model-class (probably Sonnet for scoring, possibly Opus for rewrites). The design should integrate with the existing curmudgeon/analyst loop without doubling per-section review cycles. Sloppytoppy is the audience-advocate; curmudgeon is the scientific-skeptic; analyst is the kernel-of-truth builder. Three different lenses on the same content — sloppytoppy is the reader's lens.

tasks.description: Design the scoring rubric. Operator specified two axes (length, understandability) and a target reader (flat-earth-level). Tinker fleshes out:
deliverable_specifications: Score scale (e.g., 1-10 per axis, or composite 1-100, or letter grade). Pick one and justify.
Length axis specifics: word count target per surface? Sentence count? Reading-grade-level (Flesch-Kincaid)? Some combination?

Understandability axis specifics: jargon-density? Concept-stacking depth (how many sub-claims per paragraph)? Sentence-complexity? Forward-references? Specific things a flat-earth-level reader would stumble on?
Composite formula or independent axes? If composite, how weighted? Operator's verbatim: 'shorter better, more understandable/clear better' — equal weight or one dominates?
Threshold definitions: what score = 'rewrite priority urgent' vs 'acceptable' vs 'gold standard'?

How to handle content that's intrinsically technical (e.g., WIN-038 Bessel-zero math — the math IS the argument, can't be removed). The rubric should NOT punish content for being technical when the technicality is load-bearing. Tinker needs to specify how to detect 'load-bearing technical' vs 'gratuitous technical'.

**REWRITE THRESHOLD specification** (operator amendment 2026-05-16): the rubric MUST define a stopping criterion that prevents endless minor edits. Specifically: (a) an *acceptable-floor* score above which rewrites do NOT trigger (e.g., 'if score >= 8/10, surface is acceptable, no rewrite even if not gold-standard'); (b) a *minimum-improvement-delta* — sloppytoppy must predict, before rewriting, that the post-rewrite score will improve by at least N points/levels. If predicted delta < threshold, skip the rewrite. This prevents the diminishing-returns cycle where successive minor rewrites each improve score by 0.1 forever. (c) a *cooldown window* — a surface that was rewritten in the last K days is not rewritten again unless its score has decayed below the acceptable-floor; this prevents same-surface thrash.
design_questions_for_tinker: Q1: Single composite score or two-axis (length + understandability separate)? Two-axis lets you see *which* dimension is failing on rewrites. Recommend with rationale.

Q2: How does the rubric handle the kernel-of-truth requirement (analyst.md most-important-directive)? Sloppytoppy must not score down a kernel-of-truth paragraph for being long if the length is what gives the argument credibility.
Q3: What's the calibration baseline? Score 5 current existing surfaces by hand at PROP-authoring time, use those as reference points for the rubric. Tinker picks 5 representative surfaces.

Q3b (added 2026-05-16): Recommend specific thresholds: (a) acceptable-floor (e.g., 8/10), (b) minimum-improvement-delta (e.g., +1.5 points predicted), (c) cooldown window (e.g., 14 days since last rewrite of the same surface). The recommendation should be tunable post-Phase-1 based on operator's spot-check feedback. Include the *predicted-improvement gating mechanism*: how does sloppytoppy estimate the post-rewrite score before doing the rewrite? Options: (i) heuristic delta from length-cut + jargon-replacement count, (ii) two-stage rewrite (draft → score draft → decide whether to commit), (iii) operator-approved minimum-delta-by-surface-type. Recommend with rationale.

tasks.description: Determine the unit of scoring. Operator specified 'individual wins and each of the other major sections (likely aligned to how curmudgeon broke down the phase 1 vs phase 2 work)'. Tinker maps this concretely:
deliverable_specifications: Per-WIN units: each WIN has tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, detail_claim, detail_extra. Score which fields? All? A subset?
Per-section units: data/sections.json has parts (part1.html, part2.html, etc.) — score each <h2> subsection? Each <h3>? Each <details> block?

Special surfaces: docs/index.html generated structures (ds-thesis-hero, ds-falsifiability-module, ds-verdict-bars, ds-evaluate-preface). These are generated from generate-html.js — does sloppytoppy score the source or the output?
Killshots tab, Timestamp Error tab, Predictions tab, Breaking News — each is a distinct surface with its own audience. Map each.
PROP-019 / curmudgeon Phase 1 vs Phase 2 reference: dig out what curmudgeon Phase 1 vs Phase 2 actually covers and align sloppytoppy's section-list to that breakdown.
design_questions_for_tinker: Q4: What's the total surface count after expansion? If it's >200 scorable units, batching gets tight even at large batch sizes.

Q5: Should sloppytoppy score the SOURCE data files (wins.json, sections.json) or the GENERATED docs/index.html? Source is canonical; generated reflects user-facing reality but changes with generate-html.js edits. Pick one.

tasks.description: Design the section-appropriate internal format that sloppytoppy + curmudgeon/analyst use when authoring content. Operator specified: 'consistent format that is section appropriate'. This is the load-bearing creative work of the PROP — what does the format look like?
deliverable_specifications: Per-section template definitions. For WIN cards: probably (claim → setup → kernel-of-truth → punchline → evidence). For section prose: probably (TLDR-thesis → 2-3 supporting paragraphs → punchline → forward-link). For killshot tab rows: (claim → status → one-sentence why). Tinker authors actual templates.
Style guide elements: sentence-length cap? Paragraph-length cap? Concept-per-paragraph cap? Forward-reference handling (when can you say 'see §6.14' vs when must you state the point inline)?

The format is INTERNAL — not published to readers as visible structure. So it lives in monitor/prompts/reference/sloppytoppy-format-spec.md or similar, referenced by both sloppytoppy and the authoring agents (analyst, curmudgeon).
Specifically address: how does the format help the agents writing content WITHOUT making outputs look formulaic? The format is scaffolding, not a template-fill.
design_questions_for_tinker: Q6: One unified format with per-surface variants, or per-surface formats? Recommend with rationale.

Q7: How does the format interact with the existing TLDR convention (CLAUDE.md Progressive Disclosure section)? The current TLDR is 2-3 sentence punchline-first; sloppytoppy's format should extend that, not contradict it.
Q8: Format violation handling — when curmudgeon/analyst writes content that violates the format, what happens? Sloppytoppy scores it down? Refuses to integrate (decider gate)? Logs a soft-complaint?

tasks.description: Sloppytoppy has two distinct modes: scoring and rewriting. Per operator: 'a run should be either a scoring run or a rewrite run, not both'. Tinker specifies:
deliverable_specifications: Dispatcher logic: how does sloppytoppy decide which mode to run? Likely: 'if unscored content exists OR substantially-changed-since-last-score content exists → scoring mode. Else if low-scored content exists AND no recent rewrite-attempt → rewrite mode. Else no-op.'
Mode 1 (Scoring): batching strategy. Operator said 'batch size should be reasonably large' — quantify. Tinker recommends a target (e.g., 30-50 surfaces per run).

Mode 2 (Rewriting): batching strategy. Operator said 'shouldn't need to do singletons, but needs to batch limit based on reasonable context management'. Quantify (e.g., 3-5 rewrites per run for substantive sections, 10-15 for short surfaces).

Score storage: where do scores live? Recommend: new file monitor/sloppytoppy/scores.json keyed by surface-id. Each entry: {surface_id, score_length, score_understandability, composite, last_scored_at, last_scored_run_id, content_hash_at_score_time}. Hash-mismatch on next pass triggers re-score.
Rewrite output: where? Recommend reusing analyst's expansions/ path with authored_by='sloppytoppy' and review_class='verification' (because rewrites are scoped wordsmithing, not new arguments).
design_questions_for_tinker: Q9: Schedule cadence — daily? Twice daily? Operator didn't specify. Recommend a default and explain why.

Q10: Model choice — Sonnet for scoring (mechanical text-property assessment), Opus for rewrites (judgment-heavy)? Or Sonnet for both? Recommend with rationale matching the baby-analyst (PROP-034) model-class pattern.

Q11: How does sloppytoppy interact with curmudgeon's existing review cycle? Sloppytoppy rewrites → curmudgeon adversarial review (per operator: 'curmudgeon will already do a volume based review after commit') → if curmudgeon finds a problem, what's the loopback? Open-issue against the sloppytoppy rewrite? Roll back? Curmudgeon writes a counter-rewrite?

Q12: Punt-to-curmudgeon/analyst mechanism — operator: 'can punt questions to curmudgeon/analyst in case they want to make a change but aren't sure they are doing it in a way with integrity'. What's the file/inbox? Use the existing HNOTE pattern? Dedicated sloppytoppy/integrity-punts/ directory?

phase_design_template.phase_1: Scoring-only. Sloppytoppy runs on schedule, scores surfaces, writes monitor/sloppytoppy/scores.json. No rewrites. Operator + curmudgeon review the score distribution and rubric calibration. Operator validates the rubric is correctly identifying low-scoring content. Likely 7 days.
phase_design_template.phase_1_exit_criteria: All in-scope surfaces scored at least once (full coverage). Operator agrees rubric ranking matches intuition for ~20 spot-checked surfaces (sloppytoppy's top-10-worst and bottom-10-best should match operator's gut read). Score distribution is well-formed (not bimodal-all-low or bimodal-all-high — suggests rubric is calibrated).
phase_design_template.phase_1_rollback_criteria: Rubric produces obvious misjudgments (e.g., kernel-of-truth paragraphs scored as 'verbose' when their length is load-bearing). Operator-judgment trump card; if rubric is wrong, no rewrites should fire.
phase_design_template.phase_2_conditional: Rewrites enabled. Sloppytoppy picks lowest-scored surfaces and rewrites them, batched. authored_by='sloppytoppy', review_class='verification'. Curmudgeon adversarial review follows commit per existing flow.
phase_design_template.phase_2_exit_criteria: After 14 days of rewrites: ≥50% of low-scored surfaces have improved scores (validated by re-scoring), curmudgeon flagged ≤2 sloppytoppy rewrites as inadequate-quality.
phase_design_template.phase_2_rollback_criteria: Curmudgeon flags >3 rewrites as inadequate-quality in 14 days. Operator sees readers-experience degraded (subjective but real). Sloppytoppy starts simplifying away kernel-of-truth content.
phase_design_template.phase_3_conditional: Format adoption by curmudgeon/analyst as authoring-time guidance. analyst-mode1-expansions.md and curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md reference sloppytoppy-format-spec.md when authoring new content.

tasks.description: Sloppytoppy interacts with the existing pipeline. Tinker maps the interactions:
deliverable_specifications: Analyst-baby (PROP-034) — verification-class drain agent. Sloppytoppy rewrites are also verification-class. Boundary: baby drains *open ISSs* assigned-analyst routed by decider; sloppytoppy drains *low-scored surfaces* identified by its own scoring. No overlap if defined cleanly.
Analyst (Opus) — deep-attack expansions, defense neutralization, kernel-of-truth authoring. Sloppytoppy can punt to analyst for kernel-of-truth-affecting rewrites (via task_4 Q12 mechanism).

Curmudgeon — adversarial review. Sloppytoppy rewrites enter curmudgeon's queue with review_class='verification' (batchable per PROP-025 Step 8a). DIRECTIVE-20260514-002's pending PROP would let curmudgeon-verify (Sonnet) handle these cheaply.
Decider — integration of sloppytoppy rewrites into wins.json/sections.json. No change to decider's existing flow if rewrites use the analyst/expansions/ EXP shape.
Tinker — pipeline-health audit for sloppytoppy. Add a stalled-output check for monitor/sloppytoppy/latest-sloppy-summary.txt.
design_questions_for_tinker: Q13: Naming the boundary between baby and sloppytoppy. Both are verification-class drainers, different sources. Define so future agents (if any) extend cleanly.

Q14: Should curmudgeon receive a class_hint of 'readability-rewrite' on sloppytoppy commits so it knows to skip parts of its rubric that wouldn't apply (e.g., kernel-of-truth-completeness check is upstream of sloppytoppy's scope)?

tasks.description: Steve prefers honest evaluation. Steelman the case for NOT building sloppytoppy. Strongest counter-arguments:
counter_args_to_address: 'Adding agents adds pipeline complexity. Existing agents already produce 60+ commits/day; sloppytoppy adds more.' Rebuttal expected: cost is real but the wordiness problem operator observed is real too; better to address it with an explicit lens than hope curmudgeon catches it adversarially (curmudgeon's eye is on scientific gaps, not readability).

'Curmudgeon could just add a readability rubric to its existing review.' Rebuttal expected: curmudgeon's job is adversarial-skeptic, not audience-advocate. Asking the same agent to be both creates rubric drift. Sloppytoppy as a separate lens preserves curmudgeon's role purity.

'Analyst could just author shorter content.' Rebuttal expected: analyst's prompt says 'think hard' — verbosity is a side-effect of judgment-heavy work. Adding a readability constraint to analyst dilutes the kernel-of-truth focus. Sloppytoppy as post-hoc compression preserves analyst's depth.

'Score-then-rewrite is a slow feedback loop — by the time a low score triggers a rewrite, the content may have already changed.' Rebuttal expected: hash-mismatch detection (task_4) handles re-score-on-change. Plus sloppytoppy IS slow by design — readability is a slow burn, not an urgent fix.

'Flat-earth-level reader as the target may compromise scientific credibility with scientists.' Rebuttal expected: the review's published audience is the dome author's audience (flat-earthers). Scientists already accept the dome model is wrong; the readers we're trying to reach are flat-earthers. Sloppytoppy targets the actual audience.

tasks.description: Author the PROP-NNN as DESIGN-PENDING-OPERATOR-REVIEW. Do NOT self-apply. Operator reviews proposal, then operator + tinker apply prompt edits + scheduled-task creation. Follow PROP-034 authoring shape.
tasks.deliverable: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-sloppytoppy-readability-agent.json with full design including: agent prompt outline (sloppytoppy.md draft), scoring rubric spec, internal format spec, phased deployment plan with exit/rollback criteria, integration map, counter-argument section, implementation_files list (sloppytoppy.md, scores.json, format-spec.md, schedule task, CLAUDE.md updates, build.js OWNERSHIP updates).

operator_specified_design_constraints: Run discipline: a run is EITHER scoring OR rewriting, never both. Tinker MUST design the dispatcher so the two modes can't co-fire.
Batching is mandatory: scoring batches must be 'reasonably large', rewrites must NOT do singletons but must be context-managed.
Format is INTERNAL — not visible to readers, used by sloppytoppy + curmudgeon + analyst as authoring guidance.
Sloppytoppy 'can punt to curmudgeon/analyst' for integrity-sensitive changes, but operator notes this should be RARE because curmudgeon already does volume-based review after commit.
Hash-mismatch / change-detection drives re-scoring (periodically look for unscored or substantially-changed content).
Values scientific content — rewrites must not compress away kernel-of-truth.

**Rewrite threshold (operator amendment 2026-05-16):** the rubric must define a stopping criterion that prevents endless minor edits. Three mechanisms required: (a) acceptable-floor above which rewrites don't fire; (b) minimum-improvement-delta predicted before a rewrite; (c) cooldown window for re-rewriting the same surface. See task_1 Q3b for the design ask.
Agent name is sloppytoppy (non-negotiable per operator).
in_scope_for_tinker: Read CLAUDE.md to understand the existing agent pipeline architecture
Read analyst-baby.md (PROP-034 Phase 1) as the closest existing pattern
Read 5-10 representative WIN entries by hand for rubric calibration (task_1 Q3)
Author one PROP-NNN with the full design
Author one draft of sloppytoppy.md (the agent prompt itself) as an attachment or appendix to the PROP
Author one draft of sloppytoppy-format-spec.md (the internal format) as an attachment or appendix
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT create the scheduled task. Operator does that after PROP review.
Do NOT modify any existing agent prompts directly. Format adoption by analyst/curmudgeon is Phase 3 and lands as a separate amendment.
Do NOT batch with DIRECTIVE-20260514-002 (curmudgeon split). Sloppytoppy is its own thing.
Do NOT propose changes to data/wins.json or data/sections.json. Sloppytoppy is a NEW process, not a content edit.
operator_preferences_for_proposal: Counter-argument section mandatory (per operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too')
Phased deployment with explicit exit/rollback criteria (per PROP-034 precedent)
Include the 5-surface rubric calibration sample verbatim in the PROP so operator can sanity-check the rubric on real content
Don't over-design. Phase 1 is scoring only. The rubric can be refined empirically before Phase 2 lands.
expected_artifacts: Inside the PROP: appendix with sloppytoppy.md draft (the agent prompt)
Inside the PROP: appendix with sloppytoppy-format-spec.md draft (the internal format)
Inside the PROP: appendix with sloppytoppy-rubric-calibration.json draft (5 hand-scored surfaces as rubric baseline)
